quiz.title: Grade 3 Reading Comprehension – Stories & Facts
quiz.subject: Reading
quiz.description: Read short passages and answer questions about details, main idea, and why things happen.

questions.question: Passage: The fox tried to reach the grapes on the tall vine. He jumped and jumped but could not get them. Finally, he walked away saying, "They were probably sour anyway." What does the fox do at the end?
Passage: Birds build nests in trees to keep their eggs safe and warm. They use twigs, grass, and feathers. Why do birds use feathers in nests?
Passage: Mia planted seeds in her garden. Every day she watered them and kept weeds away. Soon little plants grew. What is the main idea?
Passage: The rain came down hard all morning. By afternoon the sun came out and a rainbow appeared. What happened after the rain stopped?
Passage: Penguins cannot fly but they swim very well. They use their wings like flippers in the cold water. What does 'flippers' mean here?
Passage: Sam forgot his homework at home. He felt sad but his teacher said he could bring it tomorrow. How does Sam probably feel now?
Passage: Recycling paper helps save trees. We can turn old paper into new notebooks. Why is recycling good?
Passage: The little seed was planted in soft soil. With sun, water, and time it grew into a tall sunflower. What helped the seed grow?
Passage: Dogs bark to talk to other dogs or to warn people. A wagging tail usually means they are happy. What does a wagging tail mean?
Passage: In winter, bears sleep a lot in their dens. They do not eat much during this time. What season do bears sleep a lot?
